title: Peppery Algerian Lamb Tagine
description: A hearty and flavorful slow-cooked lamb stew made with green curry paste and a blend of aromatic spices, perfect for a comforting meal on a sick day.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the bottom of a clay or ceramic tagine or a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is softened and lightly browned, about 8 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until they are browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the green curry paste, ground cumin, ground coriander, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Pour in the chicken broth and bring the mixture to a boil.
Cover the tagine or Dutch oven and transfer it to the oven, where it will cook at 300 degrees Fahrenheit for 2 1/2 hours.
Remove the tagine from the oven and let it cool slightly before serving.
Serve the lamb and sauce over couscous or rice, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or cilantro if desired.
Cook poultry to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F before serving.
